scientific_claim: YUP was delimited to a 70-kb interval controlling carotenoid accumulation
source_reported_content: Genotyping about 3000 BC3S1 and BC3S2 individuals delimited YUP to a 70-kb interval and produced refined NILs with petal lobe carotenoid accumulation
data_type: segregating genotypes and flower pigment phenotypes
limitation: supplementary marker details require manual PDF check
source_location: Results: YUP is a noncoding locus that produces siRNAs

scientific_claim: The protein-coding genes in the YUP candidate interval were not sufficient candidates based on RNAi phenotypes
source_reported_content: RNA interference experiments of eight protein-coding genes in the 70-kb interval in M. lewisii produced no phenotypic changes in carotenoid pigmentation
limitation: knockdown efficiency and full RNAi details not visible in provided text
source_location: Results: YUP is a noncoding locus that produces siRNAs

scientific_claim: YUP is a noncoding inverted-repeat locus that produces phased 21-nt siRNAs
source_reported_content: RNA-seq revealed an about 1.3-kb transcript with no conserved ORF and an inverted repeat; sRNA-seq showed abundant 21-nt siRNAs in a phased pattern in M. lewisii and M. parishii
method: RNA-seq; BLAST; hairpin prediction; sRNA-seq; phasing analysis
study_system: Mimulus lewisii; Mimulus parishii; Mpar yupC/C NIL
limitation: phasing calculation details require supplementary method check
source_location: Results: YUP is a noncoding locus that produces siRNAs

scientific_claim: Dominant YUP transcripts from M. lewisii and M. parishii repress carotenoid accumulation when overexpressed
source_reported_content: All 30 M. lewisii YUP overexpression lines and all 35 M. parishii YUP overexpression lines showed dark pink flowers with no carotenoid accumulation; 41 M. cardinalis allele lines showed no phenotypic change
limitation: 35S overexpression may differ from endogenous expression
source_location: Results: YUP is a noncoding locus that produces siRNAs

scientific_claim: siR-RCP2 targets and cleaves RCP2 transcripts
source_reported_content: Three YUP_siR1 forms were predicted to target RCP2; 5' RLM RACE confirmed RCP2 transcript cleavage at the predicted target site
data_type: small RNA sequence; RCP2 transcript cleavage
limitation: RACE clone counts should be manually checked in figure
source_location: Results: YUP represses carotenoid accumulation through RCP2

scientific_claim: siR-RCP2 is sufficient to suppress carotenoid accumulation in transgenic lines
source_reported_content: Six of 21 35S:amiR-RCP2 lines displayed dark pink flowers without carotenoid accumulation and strong lines showed 17- to 43-fold siR-RCP2 increase
limitation: not all transgenic lines showed phenotype; likely expression-dependent
source_location: Results: YUP represses carotenoid accumulation through RCP2

scientific_claim: siR-RCP2 represses RCP2 partly through translational inhibition
source_reported_content: RCP2-YFP had weak YFP signal in petal lobes despite moderate transcript differences; siR-RCP2-resistant mRCP2-YFP showed comparable protein signal and increased carotenoids in corolla and filament
method: YFP fusion reporter; silent target-site mutation; qRT-PCR; confocal microscopy
limitation: reporter context may not fully match endogenous RCP2 regulation
source_location: Results: YUP represses carotenoid accumulation through RCP2

scientific_claim: RCP1 acts upstream of YUP and YUP acts upstream of RCP2 in floral carotenoid patterning
source_reported_content: rcp1 mutant increased siR-RCP2; RCP1 overexpression decreased siR-RCP2; rcp1 yupC/C and rcp2 yupC/C double-mutant/NIL phenotypes supported the regulatory model
limitation: direct binding of RCP1 to YUP is not reported in provided text
source_location: Results: RCP1 patterns nectar guides by repressing YUP

scientific_claim: YUP-SOLAR-PELAN superlocus originated in a Mimulus subclade and contributed to flower color diversification
source_reported_content: Synteny analysis indicates the superlocus arose in the common ancestor of sections Erythranthe and Monimanthe about 5 million years ago; overexpression of YUPL in additional Mimulus species reduced carotenoid accumulation
method: synteny analysis; genome sequencing; phylogenetic timing; transgenic overexpression
limitation: selection and speciation contribution partly integrate prior ecological/genetic evidence
source_location: Results: Origin and evolution of the YUP-SOLAR-PELAN superlocus; Discussion
notes: Reasonable inference should be separated from direct functional assays
